message:
fix(db): make v15 migration SQLite-compatible for existing databases

The previous ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with NOT NULL DEFAULT can fail in
SQLite when the table already has rows (SQLite requires a constant
default for NOT NULL columns added via ALTER TABLE, and older SQLite
versions reject it entirely). Replace with a three-step approach:

1. Add recipients_json as a nullable column
2. Backfill existing rows with '{}'
3. Create an AFTER INSERT trigger to default NULL/empty values

This ensures the migration succeeds on databases with pre-existing
message rows while maintaining the same invariant that recipients_json
is always populated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1161,7 +1161,15 @@ pub fn schema_migrations() -> Vec<Migration> {
     migrations.push(Migration::new(
         "v15_add_recipients_json_to_messages".to_string(),
         "add recipients_json column to messages table".to_string(),
-        "ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN recipients_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'".to_string(),
+        "ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN recipients_json TEXT;\
+         UPDATE messages SET recipients_json = '{}' WHERE recipients_json IS NULL OR recipients_json = '';\
+         CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS trg_messages_default_recipients_json \
+         AFTER INSERT ON messages \
+         WHEN NEW.recipients_json IS NULL OR NEW.recipients_json = '' \
+         BEGIN \
+             UPDATE messages SET recipients_json = '{}' WHERE id = NEW.id; \
+         END"
+            .to_string(),
         String::new(),
     ));
